text: 4.3g of fat. saturated fat, cholesterol, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
3.4g of carbs. sugar, glycemic index, and starch are doing most of the work. for dairy, the best versions keep the convenience without wrecking the fat profile.
11.1g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
vitamin b12 at 13% daily value, vitamin a at 4% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side: lean high-protein dairy because tests whether greek yogurt is the only clean dairy S. very filling because strong practical upside. fairly simple food because strong dairy identity without dessert drift.
the drawbacks next: can be fairly salty because practical downside. texture is divisive because not universally liked. not suitable for everyone because tolerance varies.
S tier. Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
